query: our exporter crashes opening the second project but the first one is fine — where do I even start
rationale: systematic debugging: establish a repro, step before the crash -> scientific-debugging.md / reproduction-and-bisection.md

query: I'm getting a read access violation and the pointer's memory is all 0xdddddddd, what does that mean
rationale: freed-memory fill pattern at a mapped address = use-after-free -> scientific-debugging.md

query: the packaging command crashes but it's slow — can you help me find a smaller way to trigger it
rationale: shrink to a minimal deterministic repro -> reproduction-and-bisection.md

query: this regression appeared sometime in the last 200 commits, how do I find which change broke it
rationale: git bisect on a deterministic repro -> reproduction-and-bisection.md

query: stop the team from chasing the same buffer-overwrite class of bug — what design would make it crash instantly at the bad write instead
rationale: end-of-page allocator prevents the overwrite class -> bug-taxonomy.md / instrumentation-and-checks.md

query: I keep editing things at random trying to make the crash go away and it's not working
this bug only shows up once an hour and I can't catch it in the debugger — how do I make it reproducible
rationale: intermittent: stress to raise rate, circular log, record/replay -> determinism-and-replay.md

query: add assertions so a bad precondition fails loudly at the call site instead of corrupting state later
I want to tag every allocation with file and line and check the counter is zero on shutdown to catch leaks
rationale: allocation instrumentation for leak detection -> instrumentation-and-checks.md

query: should I just never destroy the document and clean up at exit so we stop hitting these dangling-pointer crashes
rationale: prevention reflection: never-free hides bugs; proper teardown exposes them -> bug-taxonomy.md

query: design the ownership convention for our parser buffers so there are no leaks or double-frees on error paths
rationale: near-miss: lifetime/ownership design -> memory-management-guide, not finding a bug

query: free nodes safely in our lock-free stack while other threads might still be reading them
rationale: near-miss: concurrent reclamation / race correctness -> lock-free-guide

query: the update loop is too slow, profile it and tell me which cache misses to fix
rationale: near-miss: profiling for speed / cache layout -> data-oriented-design-guide

query: I have a 100% repro of a use-after-free; walk me through forming a hypothesis and confirming it before I touch the code
rationale: hypothesis, one change, confirm before fixing -> scientific-debugging.md

query: make our engine tick a deterministic function of its inputs so the same input replays the same crash every time
convert this AoS particle array to SoA so the hot loop is cache friendly
rationale: near-miss: data layout for performance -> data-oriented-design-guide
